sizing_rule.note: Exit sizing via systematic averaging: divide account equity by 100 to establish unit value, then divide 100 by number of periods to get fraction of units to exit per period. Example: 100/20 weeks = 5 units/week. This is a fixed fractional approach to position reduction.

source_rule_text: When averaging out of a position over a specified period, divide total account equity by 100 to get unit value, then divide 100 by the number of periods to determine the fraction of units to exit per period.
